name: Stefan-Boltzmann radiation balance
conventions: P/A is the radiative power per unit surface area (W/m^2)
sigma = 5.670374419 x 10^-8 W m^-2 K^-4 (Stefan-Boltzmann constant)
valid for an ideal blackbody emitter (emissivity epsilon = 1); for a grey body, multiply by epsilon
for Earth's energy balance: sigma T_eq^4 = (1 - alpha) S / 4, where alpha is planetary albedo and S is solar constant
expectedLimits: T = 0: P/A = 0 (no thermal emission at zero kelvin)
small delta T: linearised heat balance, dF/dT = 4 sigma T^3 (Planck feedback parameter)
Earth equilibrium temperature without atmosphere: T_eq = ((1-alpha) S / (4 sigma))^(1/4) ~ 255 K
references: Stefan (1879) — empirical T^4 law from radiation experiments
IPCC AR6 WG1 — Chapter 7 (Radiative Forcing and Climate Sensitivity)
